drivers.name: Daryl De Leon
drivers.bio:
Daryl De Leon is a British racing driver born on 2 August 2005 in Drogheda, Ireland, of Filipino heritage - making him the first driver of Filipino descent ever to win a BTCC race. He made a stunning debut in the British Touring Car Championship in 2025, joining West Surrey Racing to drive a BMW 330e M Sport.

De Leon wasted no time making his mark, claiming a famous maiden victory at Brands Hatch Indy Circuit in just his third race weekend. He went on to dominate the Jack Sears Trophy - the award for drivers yet to score more than one podium in the series - claiming the title with pole positions and a race win across the campaign.

His impressive maiden season earned him a second year at WSR, where he steps into a grid position that previous incumbents have used as a springboard to full title contention.

drivers.name: Tom Chilton
drivers.bio:
Tom Chilton is a British racing driver born on 15 March 1985 in Reigate, Surrey. He made his BTCC debut in 2002 at the age of 17, finishing on the podium at Donington Park in just his second race - becoming one of the youngest drivers to score a podium in the series at the time.

After years developing his craft in the BTCC, Chilton made the leap to the international stage in 2012, competing in the World Touring Car Championship for SEAT and later Citroën. He secured multiple race wins and a best championship result of fifth in 2013, establishing himself as a competitive force on the global touring car scene.

Chilton has remained a consistent BTCC front-runner across more than two decades of competition, racking up race wins and podiums for a host of manufacturers. Few drivers on the grid can match his experience, and the Surrey racer remains as competitive as ever with Team VERTU's Hyundai operation.

history.team: Bristol Street Motors with Excelr8 TradePriceCars.com
Team Shredded Wheat Racing with Gallagher
Team Shredded Wheat Racing with Gallagher

drivers.name: Ricky Collard
drivers.bio:
Ricky Collard is a British racing driver born on 30 July 1996 in Frimley, Surrey - the son of long-serving BTCC driver Rob Collard, and part of a three-generation motorsport family that stretches back to his grandfather Mick, a celebrated hot rod racer.

He made an eye-catching BTCC debut in 2018, stepping in for his injured father at West Surrey Racing and immediately claiming a podium at Silverstone. After stepping away from touring cars to pursue GT racing, Collard returned to the BTCC in 2022 with Speedworks Motorsport, completing two full seasons and finishing a career-best 8th in 2023 as the leading Toyota driver.

His most remarkable chapter came in 2024 when he and his father Rob won the British GT Championship GT3 title together as teammates at Barwell Motorsport in a Lamborghini Huracán - one of the most touching stories in recent British motorsport history. Ricky returns to the BTCC for 2026 with Team VERTU, his most competitive touring car opportunity yet.

history.team: Toyota GAZOO Racing UK with Speedworks
Toyota GAZOO Racing UK with Speedworks

drivers.name: Lewis Selby
drivers.bio:
Despite standing at more than six feet tall, Lewis Selby has become synonymous with competing in MINIs - both classic and modern. A racer defined by commitment rather than budget, Selby worked his way through the MINI world with genuine results at every level.

Winning races in the MINI Miglia Championship and MINI Se7en Challenge, he represented NAPA Racing at the 2022 Goodwood Festival of Speed before racing a NAPA-branded car in the EnduroKA endurance series. Making the switch to the MINI CHALLENGE JCW class in 2023, Selby delivered a run of standout performances to seal the coveted Directors Cup title.

Now the Buckinghamshire racer faces his biggest challenge yet, stepping up to the BTCC with NAPA Racing UK and the Ford Focus Titanium. With a wealth of front-wheel-drive experience behind him and eligibility for the Jack Sears Trophy, Selby arrives on the full-time BTCC grid with results to prove and nothing to lose.

drivers.name: Aiden Moffat
drivers.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing
drivers.bio:
Aiden Moffat is a Scottish racing driver born on 28 September 1996 in Dalkeith, near Edinburgh. He made motorsport history in 2013 when he became the youngest driver ever to start a BTCC race, lining up at Knockhill at just 16 years old. That record - set at his home circuit in front of a passionate Scottish crowd - remains one of the most celebrated in the championship's history.

Moffat spent the formative years of his career at the family-run Laser Tools Racing outfit, developing from a teenage wildcard into a race winner. He demonstrated remarkable adaptability, taking wins in front-wheel-drive machinery and rear-wheel-drive cars alike - a rare distinction in a series where very few drivers have mastered both. His best championship result came in 2021 when he finished eighth in the overall standings.

Having progressed through WSR and Toyota programmes in recent seasons, the Scotsman arrives at Power Maxed Racing for 2026 determined to rediscover the form that once made him one of the series' most watchable talents.

history.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts Racing with WSR
LKQ Euro Car Parts with SYNETIQ

drivers.name: Dexter Patterson
drivers.team: Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing
drivers.bio:
Dexter Patterson is a Scottish racing driver born on 18 August 2003 in Glasgow. A product of the karting academy system, Patterson rose rapidly through international junior motorsport and was recruited as a junior driver by the Sauber Formula 1 team - a rare vote of confidence for a teenager from the UK club racing scene.

He progressed into single-seater racing, competing in the F4 British Championship and the Euroformula Open Championship, before making the move to touring cars. He joined the British Touring Car Championship in 2022 at Laser Tools Racing, making his debut at just 18 - one of the youngest drivers in the field that season.

Still building towards his peak years, Patterson is regarded as a raw talent with the foundations to develop into a genuine BTCC front-runner. His 2026 campaign with Power Maxed Racing represents another step in that development.

history.team: Re.Beverages and Bartercard with Team HARD

drivers.name: Max Buxton
drivers.bio:
Max Buxton is a British racing driver born on 14 August 2005 in Lancashire. One of the youngest drivers on the 2026 BTCC grid, he became a full-time competitor in the championship in 2025 with Toyota Gazoo Racing UK, making his debut at just 19 years old.

Buxton arrived in the BTCC after cutting his teeth in junior saloon car categories, building experience in UK competition before earning his opportunity at the top of British touring car racing. His debut season was spent learning the circuits and the demands of racing in one of the world's most competitive tin-top series, gaining valuable mileage against drivers with years of BTCC experience.

Now with Speedworks Corolla Racing for 2026, Buxton looks to translate that first-season knowledge into stronger results and begin making his mark on a championship that has produced some of Britain's finest racing talent.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA

drivers.name: Chris Smiley
drivers.bio:
Chris Smiley is a Northern Irish racing driver born on 6 April 1992 in Larne, County Antrim. He began his BTCC career in 2016 and quickly graduated from the back of the grid to a competitive race winner, claiming his maiden BTCC victory in 2018 driving a Honda Civic for BTC Norlin Racing.

Away from the BTCC, Smiley demonstrated his adaptability by winning the TCR UK Championship in 2022 - proving he remains one of the most versatile touring car drivers in Britain. He returned to the BTCC in 2024 with Restart Racing and has been building momentum with the Hyundai ever since.

Known for a tenacious, never-give-up style that has earned him the respect of the paddock, Smiley represents the best of Northern Irish motorsport and lines up for 2026 with a renewed hunger to add to his BTCC win tally.

drivers.name: Dan Cammish
drivers.bio:
Dan Cammish is a British racing driver born on 10 April 1989 in Harrogate, Yorkshire. Before reaching the BTCC, he made his name in Formula Ford, winning the British Formula Ford Championship in 2014 in dominant fashion - a title that has historically served as the launchpad for countless successful careers before him.

Cammish joined the BTCC in 2018 with the Honda-powered Halfords Yuasa Racing team and immediately impressed, scoring race wins in his debut season. He went on to finish third in the championship in both 2019 and 2020, cementing his status as one of the leading front-wheel-drive specialists in the series.

Now with NAPA Racing UK and the Ford Focus, Cammish brings consistent top-five championship pace and a relentless qualifying record to a team built around winning. He remains one of the most technically precise drivers in the series and one of the most formidable challengers whenever he is given competitive machinery.

drivers.name: Nicolas Hamilton
drivers.bio:
Nicolas Hamilton is a British racing driver born on 28 March 1992 in Stevenage, Hertfordshire - the younger half-brother of seven-time Formula One World Champion Lewis Hamilton. Nic made history as the first disabled driver to compete in the BTCC, racing with cerebral palsy, a condition that affects his lower body and requires him to use hand controls in place of traditional pedals.

He made his BTCC debut in 2015 as a guest driver with AmD Tuning.com and has returned to the series on multiple occasions since, accumulating over 160 race starts and becoming one of the most recognised and popular faces in British motorsport. His determination to compete at the highest level - and to do so not as a sideshow but as a genuine racer - has earned enormous respect across the entire paddock.

For 2026, Hamilton joins Team VERTU in partnership with Draper Tools and Excelr8 Motorsport, lining up in a Hyundai i30 Fastback N alongside teammates who have fought for championship wins. It is the most competitive drive of his career and one he intends to make count.

history.team: ROKiT Racing with iQuoto Online Trading

drivers.name: Daniel Rowbottom
drivers.bio:
Daniel Rowbottom is a British racing driver born on 18 February 1989 in Kidderminster, Worcestershire. He worked his way up through club and saloon car racing before landing a BTCC debut in 2019 with Ciceley Motorsport - a season that showed enough promise to keep him in the series.

The breakthrough came in 2021 when Rowbottom scored his first BTCC race win at Croft Circuit driving for the Cataclean-backed Halfords Racing Honda operation. That result proved the turning point in a career that had threatened to plateau, and he has since developed into a consistent race winner and front-running contender.

His 2025 season with NAPA Racing UK was perhaps his finest to date - three wins and a fifth-place finish in the overall standings - establishing him firmly among the series' top tier. Now with Cataclean Plato Racing and the Mercedes A35 Saloon for 2026, Rowbottom arrives hungry to continue that upward trajectory.

drivers.name: Adam Morgan
drivers.bio:
Adam Morgan is a British racing driver born on 28 October 1988 in Blackburn, Lancashire. One of the BTCC's longest-serving competitors, he made his debut in 2012 with Speedworks Motorsport and has since become a permanent fixture in British touring car racing across more than a decade of continuous competition.

Morgan established himself as one of the most accomplished drivers in rear-wheel-drive machinery, squeezing results from Mercedes-Benz machinery that consistently punched above its weight. He took multiple race wins between 2014 and 2022 and finished as high as seventh in the championship, demonstrating that longevity and racecraft are virtues that can compete with outright machinery advantage.

For 2026 he switches to Cataclean Plato Racing and the Mercedes A35 Saloon - a car built around a driver who knows exactly how to get the best from Stuttgart iron. With well over 400 BTCC race starts to his name, Morgan brings an unmatched depth of experience to a squad aiming to return to the sharp end of the grid.

history.team: Carlube TripleR Racing with Mac Tools

drivers.name: Árón Taylor-Smith
drivers.team: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport
drivers.bio:
Árón Taylor-Smith is a Northern Irish racing driver born on 9 November 1989 in Ballymena, County Antrim. After breaking into the BTCC in 2011, he quickly established himself as an entertaining front-runner, winning races and competing at international level in the World Touring Car Championship.

Taylor-Smith spent several seasons away from the BTCC before returning to the series and showing immediately that the speed had not dimmed. He claimed race wins and multiple podiums across his career, including a stint as the BTCC's leading independent driver, and has remained one of the more tenacious and unpredictable talents in the paddock.

Now with Laser Tools Racing for 2026, Taylor-Smith lines up in the Toyota Corolla alongside three-time BTCC champion Gordon Shedden - forming a pairing with over three decades of combined touring car experience between them.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA
Team HARD. with HUB Financial Solutions

drivers.name: Gordon Shedden
drivers.team: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport
drivers.bio:
Gordon Shedden is a Scottish racing driver born on 15 February 1979 in Edinburgh, and one of the most decorated champions in British Touring Car Championship history. Known throughout the paddock as 'Flash', he made his BTCC debut in 2001 and developed into one of the series' most formidable operators over the following decade.

Shedden won the BTCC Drivers' Championship three times - in 2012, 2015 and 2016 - all driving for Honda under Team Dynamics alongside teammate Matt Neal. The trio of titles came during what many regard as the greatest era of Honda dominance in the series, and cemented his reputation as one of the modern greats of touring car racing.

After a period away, he returned to the BTCC in 2021 and immediately proved he had lost nothing, winning races in 2021, 2022 and 2025. Now into his mid-40s, 'Flash' continues to compete with the same smooth, measured precision that made him a champion - and remains one of the most respected and formidable figures in the touring car paddock.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA

drivers.name: Josh Cook
drivers.bio:
Josh Cook is a British racing driver born on 15 June 1991 in Shepton Mallet, Somerset. He made his BTCC debut in 2015 with Power Maxed Racing and quickly developed a reputation for spectacular racing, aggressive overtaking and a genuinely fan-pleasing approach that has made him one of the series' most popular drivers.

Cook came into his own at BTC Racing, where he spent several seasons establishing himself as a genuine front-runner and racking up race wins alongside multiple Independent Championship titles. His 2021 campaign - third in the overall standings with five race wins - was arguably his finest season to date and underlined his potential to challenge for the outright title with the right machinery.

Now with Speedworks Corolla Racing and the Toyota Corolla GR Sport, Cook brings a proven ability to win races in any conditions. On his day - particularly at circuits like Thruxton and in wet weather - he remains among the most difficult drivers in the championship to beat.

history.team: Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with IAA
LKQ Euro Car Parts with SYNETIQ
Team Parker Racing with Maximum Motorsport

drivers.name: Sam Osborne
drivers.bio:
Sam Osborne is a British racing driver born on 24 March 1993 in Worksop, Nottinghamshire. He made his BTCC debut in 2019 and spent the early part of his career working through the lower-ranked teams and midfield machinery before finding a long-term home with NAPA Racing UK from 2023 onwards.

The move to Motorbase's NAPA operation transformed his results, giving him the Ford Focus ST tools to compete at the sharp end of the field. That faith was rewarded in 2025 when Osborne claimed his maiden BTCC race win - a moment that had been building across six seasons of persistent development and learning.

Staying with NAPA Racing UK for 2026, Osborne is a driver who has learned the BTCC the hard way and is all the more determined for it. With the best equipment of his career beneath him and a race win already under his belt, he enters the season with a genuine belief that the first victory can be the first of many.

history.team: MB Motorsport accelerated by Blue Square

drivers.name: Tom Ingram
drivers.bio:
Tom Ingram is a British racing driver born on 20 August 1993 in High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire. He began his touring car journey with Speedworks Motorsport and the Toyota Avensis in 2014, building steadily through the midfield before developing into one of the championship's leading lights.

Ingram became BTCC champion for the first time in 2022 with Excelr8 Motorsport, having spent years as one of the series' most consistent front-runners. But the 2025 title - secured with Team VERTU in a dominant season that yielded seven race wins and eighteen podiums - confirmed him as the outstanding driver of his generation and the man to beat on any circuit, in any conditions.

Fast, precise and clinical under pressure, Ingram approaches touring car racing with an economy of effort that belies genuine pace. Now defending champion and into his second decade in the BTCC, he lines up for 2026 as the standard everyone else is chasing.

history.team: Bristol Street Motors with Excelr8 TradePriceCars.com
Toyota Gazoo Racing UK with Ginsters

drivers.name: Mikey Doble
drivers.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing
drivers.bio:
Mikey Doble is a British racing driver born on 8 December 1998 in Caterham, Surrey. He made his BTCC debut in 2023 with Power Maxed Racing, stepping up to one of the most competitive touring car championships in the world after honing his craft in junior saloon car categories.

After a measured first season spent adapting to the demands of BTCC machinery and close-quarters combat against far more experienced drivers, Doble claimed his maiden BTCC race win in 2025 - an important milestone that showed the potential those around him in the paddock had always believed was there.

Still only in his mid-20s and continuing with LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing for 2026, Doble is a driver on a clear upward curve. The BTCC has a long history of producing champions who needed three or four seasons to find their footing - and the signs suggest Doble may well be following that same path.

history.team: Motor Parts Direct with Power Maxed Racing

teams.name: Motor Parts Direct with Power Maxed Racing
Motor Parts Direct with Power Maxed Racing

drivers.name: Charles Rainford
drivers.bio:
Charles Rainford is a British racing driver born on 20 July 1998 in Horsham, West Sussex. He cut his teeth in historic motorsport and the Porsche Carrera Cup GB - claiming the Pro-Am class title in 2022 and finishing runner-up overall in 2024 - before West Surrey Racing gambled on a largely unknown quantity and handed him a BTCC seat for 2025.

That gamble paid off spectacularly. Rainford took a maiden BTCC victory in just his sixth race start at Brands Hatch Indy, added a pole position at Knockhill and finished eighth in the Drivers' Championship - one of the most striking debut seasons the series has seen in years. He was also voted the 2025 Fans' Choice Award winner, recognition of the impression he had made on both the paddock and the public alike.

For 2026, Rainford stays with WSR and arrives as one of the championship's most anticipated stories. Quick, composed and seemingly immune to the pressure that comes with expectation, he is a genuine title contender in the making.

history.team: LKQ Euro Car Parts Racing with WSR

drivers.name: Ashley Sutton
drivers.bio:
Ashley Sutton is a British racing driver born on 15 January 1994 in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire. He made his BTCC debut in 2016 with MG Racing and, in one of the most audacious rookie title wins the championship has ever seen, returned the following year to claim the Drivers' Championship at his first full attempt - driving a rear-wheel-drive Subaru Levorg estate against the front-wheel-drive establishment.

That 2017 title was just the beginning. Sutton added further championships in 2020 and 2021 with Laser Tools Racing's Infiniti Q50, establishing himself as the dominant force of the era, before joining NAPA Racing UK and claiming a fourth title in 2023 in one of the most complete seasons the championship has ever witnessed - twelve victories, twenty podiums and ten pole positions in a single year.

The most successful active driver on the BTCC grid, Sutton lines up for 2026 as the hunter rather than the hunted - driven by a fierce desire to add a fifth title and cement a legacy that already stands comparison with the greatest the series has ever produced.

drivers.name: James Dorlin
drivers.bio:
James Dorlin is a British racing driver born on 18 April 1999 in Barnsley, South Yorkshire. He emerged as a standout junior talent, winning the Ginetta Junior Championship in 2016 at the age of just 17, and went on to build a substantial career in GT machinery before making the leap to touring cars.

Dorlin raced extensively in the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and British GT, developing the racecraft and consistency that long-distance and endurance formats demand, before earning his BTCC debut in 2025 with Toyota Gazoo Racing UK - what proved an educational first season in one of the world's most demanding touring car categories.

For 2026, he joins Restart Racing with the Hyundai i30N - a package capable of winning races - and arrives with the hunger of a driver who spent years preparing for this moment. His GT versatility and technical maturity give him tools that drivers fresh from single-seaters simply do not have.

history.team: TOYOTA GAZOO Racing UK with IAA

teams.name: NAPA Racing UK
teams.bio:
Operating under the Motorbase Performance banner, NAPA Racing UK are one of the most formidable outfits in the BTCC paddock. Founded in 2006 by David Bartrum in Sittingbourne, Kent, the team have built a reputation for extracting the maximum from Ford-powered machinery over two decades of competition.

The squad claimed back-to-back Drivers' and Teams' Championships in 2024 and 2025 with Jake Hill and Ashley Sutton respectively, cementing their status as the dominant force in the modern era. Their 2025 title haul, a clean sweep of Drivers', Teams' and Manufacturers' titles, was the team's most complete season to date.

For 2026 the team field four cars under the NAPA Auto Parts livery, running a quartet of highly experienced racers led by two-time champion Ashley Sutton. The distinctive yellow and blue colour scheme has become one of the most recognised sights in British motorsport.

teams.name: Team VERTU
teams.bio:
Run by the award-winning EXCELR8 Motorsport, Team VERTU are backed by one of the UK's largest motor retail groups. EXCELR8 entered the BTCC in 2018 and rapidly grew into championship contenders, earning their first major drivers' titles in 2022 and 2025 when Tom Ingram claimed the Drivers' Championship.

Based in Huntingdon, Cambridgeshire, the team have developed a fierce reputation for consistently fast and reliable Hyundai i30N Fastback machinery. Ingram remained their spearhead throughout their Hyundai era before the operation transitioned to new machinery for 2026.

The VERTU Motors sponsorship has given the squad significant commercial backing, and the team's data-led approach under team principal Justina Williams has seen them become one of the best-resourced teams on the grid. With multiple championship wins across different categories, EXCELR8's move into BTCC has been one of the success stories of the past decade.

teams.name: Speedworks Corolla Racing
teams.bio:
Speedworks Motorsport are the team behind the Toyota Corolla GR Sport BTCC programme, and one of the most respected independent operations in British touring car racing. Founded in 2010 and based in Middlewich, Cheshire, they made their full-time BTCC debut in 2013 before growing steadily into title contenders.

The team's crowning moment came in 2022 when Tom Ingram, their long-serving lead driver, won the Drivers' Championship after years of consistent front-running. That title validated years of careful development and cemented Speedworks' standing as a major force in the series.

Running the Toyota Corolla GR Sport under the GR Sport Corolla Racing banner, Speedworks benefit from a strong manufacturer relationship with Toyota GB and are one of the few teams to develop and run their own car from the ground up. Their Middlewich base is home to a meticulous engineering operation that punches well above its weight relative to its size.

teams.name: WSR
teams.bio:
West Surrey Racing are the most decorated and longest-running team in the BTCC. Founded in 1979 by Dick Bennetts, the Wokingham-based outfit have been a constant presence in British motorsport for over four decades, fielding cars for champions including Steve Soper, Will Hoy, James Thompson, Colin Turkington and Rob Collard.

The team's association with BMW stretches back many years and they are regarded as the factory BMW partner in the BTCC, running the iconic 330e M Sport and latterly the 128ti. Their engineering department is widely considered to be among the finest in the paddock and they have historically been the benchmark against which rival teams measure themselves.

With nine Drivers' Championship victories and five Teams' titles to their name, WSR's record is unmatched in the modern era of the championship. Colin Turkington alone won four titles with the team, making him the most successful driver in BTCC history. The team continues to attract blue-chip sponsorship and remains one of the best-funded and best-resourced operations in the series.

teams.name: LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing
teams.bio:
Power Maxed Racing are one of the most ambitious independent teams in the BTCC, based at their Coventry headquarters in the heart of the UK's motorsport corridor. Shaun Hollamby's team made their BTCC debut in 2010 and have spent subsequent years steadily building their technical capability.

The team have campaigned a variety of machinery over the years, most recently switching to the Audi S3 Saloon as part of the Goodyear-sponsored PMR programme. Their Coventry base houses an in-house engineering and fabrication department, allowing the team to react quickly to the demands of the BTCC's intensive race calendar.

Power Maxed are known for nurturing young British talent and have given a number of drivers their first full-time BTCC opportunities. The team's stated ambition is to secure their first outright championship title, and they continue to develop their package in pursuit of that goal.

teams.name: Cataclean Plato Racing
teams.bio:
Plato Racing is the team founded by BTCC legend Jason Plato, one of the most successful drivers in the history of the championship with two Drivers' titles and over 100 race wins to his name. After retiring from full-time driving, Plato channelled his experience and passion for the series into running his own outfit.

Based in Kidlington, Oxfordshire, the team campaign Mercedes-AMG A35 Saloon machinery and represent one of the most intriguing projects on the 2026 grid. Plato's hands-on involvement, drawing on his decades of experience as a front-running driver, gives the team a unique perspective on car setup and driver coaching.

With Plato's unrivalled knowledge of what it takes to win in the BTCC, the team are building methodically towards the front of the field. Their Mercedes-AMG represents one of the newer platforms in the series, and Plato Racing are working hard to develop the package into a consistent championship challenger.

teams.name: Restart Racing
teams.bio:
Restart Racing carry forward a storied history in the modern BTCC, operating from their base in Didcot, Oxfordshire. The team were originally known as BTC Racing, later running as Rich Energy BTC Racing, before rebranding as Restart Racing ahead of their switch to Hyundai machinery.

Under their BTC Racing identity, the team became one of the series' top outfits, consistently challenging for race wins and podiums with drivers including Josh Cook and Jake Hill. The operation rebranded as Restart Racing ahead of their switch to Hyundai machinery for 2026, marking a new chapter in their professional development.

Founded in 2000, the team have accumulated over two decades of BTCC experience and have never been far from the sharp end of the championship. For 2026 the Restart Racing brand represents a fresh identity, and the team are well-positioned to challenge at the front of the grid with experienced drivers and strong manufacturer support.

teams.name: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport
teams.bio: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport are a competitive independent outfit campaigning Toyota Corolla GR Sport machinery in the BTCC. Backed by Laser Tools, one of the UK's leading hand tool suppliers, the team's partnership with MB Motorsport brings proven BTCC expertise to the operation.

teams.name: Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing
teams.bio:
Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing is the second BTCC entry run by Shaun Hollamby's Coventry-based Power Maxed Racing operation, carrying the Steel Seal head gasket repair brand as title sponsor. PMR have been a fixture in the BTCC since 2010, and their multi-car programme for 2026 reflects the team's growing ambition and commercial support.

Running Audi A3 Saloon machinery, the Steel Seal entry campaigns alongside their LKQ Euro Car Parts-backed sister car as part of a coordinated effort to score as many constructor points as possible across the season.
